news.summary: Nutrien Ltd. revealed its Q1 2025 financial report today, posting a net profit of $19 million, translating to diluted net earnings per share of $0.02. The adjusted EBITDA for the same period stood at $0.9 billion, with adjusted net earnings per share recorded at $0.11. The company witnessed robust potash sales volumes in the first quarter, along with heightened ammonia operating rates, setting up its retail network for an anticipated successful spring planting season in North America.
news.headline: Nutrien Ltd. reports Q1 2025 results: net earnings $19M, $0.9B adjusted EBITDA, $0.11 adjusted EPS, strong sales volumes.

news.summary: In Toronto, XTM Inc. (QB: XTMIF / CSE: PAID / FSE: 7XT), a fintech company specializing in automated tip calculations, instant payouts, and earned wage access, has released its audited financial statements and management’s discussion and analysis for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2024. news.summary: Plaza Retail REIT reported its financial results for the quarter ending March 31, 2025. They acquired full ownership of Tacoma Plaza, converting 40,000 sq ft into a grocery store for a $1 million incremental NOI. Another grocery store deal and construction on excess land are expected to add $700,000 in NOI. Selected IFRS financial results showed a 5.3% increase in revenue to $31,137, NOI of $18.3 million, and a net profit of $9.3 million. Selected non-IFRS results included FFO of $9.8 million, AFFO of $8.3 million, and a same-asset NOI increase of 1.5%. The company's strategy of optimize, intensify, and consolidate has shown positive results. Explanations for the financial changes were provided in the article.
news.headline: Plaza Retail REIT had strong Q1 2025 results: $31.1M revenues, $18.3M NOI, $9.3M profit. Focused strategy driving growth.

news.summary: Flutter Entertainment, a leading online sports betting and iGaming operator, reported their Q1 results and updated their 2025 guidance. In Q1 2025, they saw an 8% increase in average monthly players to 14,880, generating $3.67 billion in revenue and a net income of $335 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $616 million. The US business revenue grew by 18%, reaching $1.67 billion, with sportsbook revenue up 15% and iGaming up 32%. International revenue was stable with Southern Europe and Africa leading in growth. Flutter anticipates a 22% increase in 2025 revenue to $17.08 billion, led by the US business, with adjusted EBITDA expected to reach $3.18 billion. CEO Peter Jackson highlighted growth in the US and international markets, strategic priorities, and shareholder value creation. The company maintains a strong capital structure, targeting a leverage ratio of 2.0-2.5x, and plans to return up to $1 billion cash to shareholders in 2025. For more details, the full article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Flutter Entertainment, leading online betting and iGaming operator, reports Q1 2025 results: Revenue $3.67B (+8%), Net income $335M (+289%), Adjusted EBITDA $616M (+20%). CEO optimistic about US business performance and strategic priorities. Full year 2025 guidance updated to $17.08B revenue and $3.18B adjusted EBITDA.

news.summary: Beyond Meat Inc (NASDAQ: BYND) released its first-quarter financial results after market close, with Q1 revenue totaling $68.73 million, falling short of the estimated $75.01 million. The Q1 adjusted loss was 67 cents per share, higher than the estimated 48 cents. Total revenue saw a 9.1% decrease compared to last year, attributed to legal expenses, increased provisions for inventory items, and pausing operations in China. The company reported challenges as the first quarter of 2025 unfolded. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Beyond Meat reported Q1 Revenue of $68.73M, down 9.1% YoY. Adjusted Loss: 67 cents (vs. est. 48 cents). Shares slide.

news.summary: Stocks experienced an increase in value despite concerns of stagflation from the Federal Reserve, following President Trump's decision to lift some restrictions on artificial intelligence chips. Towards the end of the trading day, MercadoLibre and AppLovin saw significant jumps in their stock prices. Post the news of Trump's action on chips and comments from Fed Chair Powell, Dow Jones Futures pointed towards a rise in stock value. MercadoLibre and AppLovin notably surged in late trading.
news.headline: Stocks rise after Trump lifts AI chip curbs, Fed Powell's comments; MercadoLibre, AppLovin soar, despite stagflation fears.

news.summary: In Chicago, the Board of Directors of ADM (NYSE: ADM) has approved a cash dividend of 51.0 cents per share for the company's common stock. Shareholders of record on May 21, 2025, will receive the payment on June 11, 2025. This marks the 374th consecutive quarterly dividend for ADM, maintaining an uninterrupted dividend streak spanning over 93 years. As of March 31, 2025, there are 480,443,947 shares of ADM common stock outstanding. ADM focuses on harnessing the potential of nature to enhance the overall quality of life for consumers.
news.headline: ADM declares cash dividend of 51.0 cents per share on common stock, payable on June 11, 2025. ADM has 480,443,947 outstanding shares and a 93-year dividend record.

news.summary: SmartCentres Real Estate Investment Trust reported a strong start to 2025 with a $7.4 million increase in net operating income and a 4.1% rise in Same Properties NOI compared to Q1 2024. The Trust leased 178,408 sq ft of existing space and signed approximately 28,620 sq ft for new-build retail in the quarter. It renewed 68% of leases with an 8.4% rental growth (excluding anchors). Walmart took possession of a 110,000 sq ft space in Oakville Centre. In terms of development, projects under construction are progressing well, with Phase I of Vaughan NW Townhomes closing 90% pre-sold units, resulting in a $12.4 million profit. Financially, net rental income for Q1 2025 was $136.8 million, up 4.6% YoY, and FFO per Unit was $0.56 (+17% YoY). The NAV per Unit was $35.51, and the debt-to-asset ratio was 44.1%. Key operational figures included a 98.4% occupancy rate and a 4.1% increase in Same Property NOI. Overall, the Trust's strong financial and operational performance sets a positive tone for its future growth and profitability.
news.headline: SmartCentres REIT had a positive first quarter in 2025 with $7.4 million increase in NOI, 4.1% SPNOI growth, and $12.4 million profit from Vaughan NW Townhomes project. Total properties owned: 196 with 98.4% occupancy rate. FFO per Unit for Q1 2025 was $0.56, representing a 17% increase. Net rental income for Q1 2025 was $136.8 million, a 4.6% increase from the same period in 2024, primarily due to lease-up and renewal activities. Net loss improved by $11.6 million. Total investment properties valued at $10,617,787. FFO was $101,919 and AFFO was $98,426 for Q1 2025. 4 self-storage facilities under construction opening in Q2 2025, and 3 more in 2026. Tower A of ArtWalk condo complex 93% pre-sold. Trust's flagship Canadian Tire store construction on track for possession in Q2 2026. NAV per Unit - diluted was $35.51. Weighted average interest rate was 3.93%. FFO per Unit was $0.56, FFO with adjustments per Unit was $0.54. Net loss per Unit was $0.05. Payout Ratio to AFFO was 83.8%. Selected Consolidated Operational, Mixed-Use Development, and Financial Information available.

news.summary: OceanaGold Corporation reported its financial and operational results for the first quarter of 2025. The company produced 117,400 ounces of gold and 3,400 tonnes of copper, which increased by 12% from the prior corresponding quarter. The All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) for gold was $1,796 per ounce. OceanaGold had a Net profit of $101 million and EPS of $0.14, with an EBITDA Margin of 53%. They also generated strong Free Cash Flow of $69 million, with zero debt and a cash balance of $228 million. The company repurchased 7.0 million common shares for $20 million and approved up to $100 million in share repurchases for the full year 2025. OceanaGold declared a $0.01 per share quarterly dividend payable in June 2025. The company is exploring a potential share consolidation to comply with minimum trading price requirements of a U.S. exchange. The Waihi North Project in New Zealand is expected to be approved by year end 2025, and new discoveries like the Pisces mineralization at Haile are promising. Shareholders can participate in Computershare's international payments service for different currency dividend payments. The company's Annual General and Special Meeting for shareholder approval of strategic actions will be held virtually on June 4, 2025. This quarter's results show OceanaGold's strong performance and financial flexibility for both growth investments and increased shareholder returns.
news.headline: OceanaGold Reports First Quarter 2025 Operating & Financial Results

news.summary: Economists are raising concerns about the possibility of stagflation in the U.S. economy, which is characterized by slow growth, rising unemployment, and persistent inflation. Following the May Federal Open Market Committee meeting, Chair Jerome Powell's remarks indicated a recognition of increased risks of higher unemployment and inflation. This development has complicated the Federal Reserve's policy decisions. Mohamed El-Erian of Allianz highlighted the Fed's acknowledgment of stagflationary risks, while Justin Wolfers from the University of Michigan expressed worries about the central bank's ability to address both higher unemployment and inflation simultaneously. Financial markets responded by reducing expectations for a June rate cut, with the probability dropping to 23% from 28% before Powell's comments. This marks a significant shift in market sentiment towards monetary easing.
news.headline: Economists warn of stagflation risk to Fed policy as US faces rare trifecta of sluggish growth, rising unemployment, and persistent inflation. Markets rethink rate cut timeline.

news.summary: BNB Chain, affiliated with Binance, saw a revival in 2023 due to increasing institutional and DeFi adoption, benefitting from significant inflows into DeFi and rising trading volumes on Binance. The native BNB token hit all-time highs in Q1 2025, even amid a broader crypto market downturn, trading at around $600 with a market cap of $85 billion as of May 7. Standard Chartered predicts the token could reach $2,775 by 2028. BNB Chain’s TVL stands at over $6 billion, rising from $3.5 billion in early 2024. It is ranked fourth among layer-1 blockchain networks by TVL. Institutional adoption is also growing, with a significant volume of the USD1 stablecoin issued on BNB Chain. Binance's trading volume reached an all-time high of $76 trillion in 2024 and accounts for 40% of spot trading globally.
news.headline: BNB Chain rebounds amid institutional, DeFi adoption, with BNB token at $600, market cap $85B. Standard Chartered predicts $2,775 by 2028.
